hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point jump signals real money betting the shutdown gets resolved before the deadline, likely driven by growing market belief that fiscal brinkmanship ends in typical last-minute compromise rather than extended crisis. At 63%, you're paying too much for a resolution that's become the base case, Republicans will fold closer to the wire, and historical shutdown patterns suggest late March panic buying drives this higher before settling.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Market Pricing in Real Endgame Risk.
daily_take.deck: The 62.9% odds on a post-March 31 shutdown jumped hard in the last 24 hours, signaling fresh confidence that Congress will actually let it happen. This smells like informed flow. Smart money sees the political math breaking down: Democrats want leverage on something, Republicans are dug in, and March 31 is closer than it feels. The market's probably right. If you don't have a position yet, lean into the YES side before this pops to 75%.

sidebar.headline: Anduril dropped 22 points hard. Either the IPO window is closing or the market got spooked by macro. Oversold if defense spending stays strong.
